description: Supply-side recruitment section with /work-with-us hub, /jobs programmatic job board, Claude Agent SDK scraping agent, and /admin portal for managing listings.

userStories.title: Job data schema and utility functions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a structured job data format and utility functions so that all downstream pages and features can load and display listings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/types/job.ts with Job interface: slug, title, category (union of 8 types), description, skills (string[]), compensationMin, compensationMax, employmentType ('CONTRACTOR'), locationType ('TELECOMMUTE'), locationRequirements (optional string), datePosted (ISO string), validThrough (ISO string), featured (boolean), archived (boolean, default false), faqs ({question, answer}[])
Create src/data/jobs/ directory
Create src/lib/jobs.ts with: getAllJobs(opts?: { includeArchived?: boolean }) returning Job[], getJobBySlug(slug: string) returning Job | null, getJobsByCategory(category: string) returning Job[]
getAllJobs defaults to excluding archived jobs, admin passes includeArchived: true
Functions read from src/data/jobs/*.json files
Add 3 sample JSON files in src/data/jobs/ for testing (one active, one archived, one featured)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. Types, 3 sample jobs, and utility functions all verified.

userStories.title: Seed job content generation script
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a script that generates 50 varied job listings from templates so that the job board launches with realistic content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/generate-seed-jobs.ts runnable with npx tsx scripts/generate-seed-jobs.ts
Templates for all 8 categories: data-labeling, quality-review, video-capture, gaming, rlhf, red-teaming, coding-review, vision-annotation
Each category has 5-8 title variations and 3-4 description templates
Descriptions are 3-4 paragraphs, answer-first format, no generic AI-ish intros (no 'In today's fast-paced world', no 'Are you ready to', no 'dive into')
Skills drawn from relevant skill pools per category (e.g., rlhf: ['preference ranking', 'LLM evaluation', 'prompt engineering'])
Compensation normalized: Entry $20-35/hr, Standard $35-55/hr, Expert $55-100/hr
datePosted randomized across last 30 days, validThrough set 3 months after
Each job has unique slug derived from title (kebab-case)
Output: 50 JSON files in src/data/jobs/
No two jobs have identical titles or descriptions
Run the script to generate all 50 seed jobs
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent to verify content quality and anti-AI-ness
userStories.notes: Completed. 50 seed jobs generated across 8 categories, all unique titles/descriptions, anti-AI-ness verified.

userStories.title: Add Work With Us to site-wide navigation
userStories.description: As a site visitor, I want to see a Work With Us link in the main navigation so that annotator recruitment is discoverable from any page.
acceptanceCriteria: Add { href: '/work-with-us', label: 'Work With Us' } to navLinks in Header.tsx
Nav order: Training Data, Expert Labeling, Work With Us, [Book a Call CTA]
Link appears in both desktop nav and mobile menu
Update Footer.tsx to include Work With Us link in navigation section
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — check desktop and mobile nav
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. Header and Footer both updated. Nav order correct.

userStories.title: Create /work-with-us hub page hero and layout
userStories.description: As a potential annotator visiting the site, I want to immediately understand what Claru offers and how to apply so that I can decide if this opportunity is right for me.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/work-with-us/page.tsx
Page metadata: title 'Work With Claru | AI Annotation Jobs & Expert Data Labeling', description targeting 'AI annotation jobs remote'
Hero section with headline, subheadline, and primary CTA linking to https://app.claru.ai/signup
Uses shared Header (with opaque prop) and Footer components
ASCII/terminal aesthetic: dark bg, green accents (#00ff88), monospace fonts (JetBrains Mono)
TextScramble effect on main heading
FadeIn scroll animations on sections
Responsive: renders correctly on mobile, tablet, and desktop
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. Page + layout created with hero, breadcrumbs, TextScramble, placeholder sections for US-005-008.

userStories.title: Work categories and domain expert segmentation sections
userStories.description: As a potential annotator, I want to see what types of work are available and which expert domains Claru needs so that I can assess if my skills are a match.
acceptanceCriteria: Add work categories section to /work-with-us with 4 cards: Data Labeling, Quality Review, Video Capture, Gaming
Each card has icon (Lucide), title, short description, Apply link to app.claru.ai/signup

Add domain expert segmentation section with 6 specialized areas: RLHF & Preference Ranking, Video Annotation, Red Teaming & Safety, Robotics & Manipulation, Vision-Language Evaluation, Coding & STEM Review
Cards use existing patterns: hover lift, border glow, dark card with border
Section links to /jobs with Browse [category] roles → links
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. 4 work category cards + 6 domain expert items with animations, hover effects, links.

userStories.title: How it Works and Why Claru sections
userStories.description: As a potential annotator, I want to understand the application process and why Claru is different from marketplace competitors so that I feel confident applying.
acceptanceCriteria: Add How it Works section with 4 numbered steps: 01 Apply, 02 Profile Review, 03 Get Matched, 04 Start Earning
Steps use monospace numbering matching existing site design patterns
Add Why Claru section contrasting marketplace model vs embedded team model
Copy should use disqualification pattern: 'You're a good fit if...' / 'This isn't for you if...'
Bottom CTA: Apply Now linking to app.claru.ai/signup
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. 4-step How it Works with connecting lines + Why Claru comparison + fit/not-fit disqualification block.

userStories.title: FAQ section with AEO schema markup on /work-with-us
userStories.description: As a search engine or AI system, I need structured FAQ content on the hub page so that Claru can appear in featured snippets and People Also Ask boxes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add FAQ section to /work-with-us with 6-8 questions: 'Is data annotation work legitimate?', 'How much do AI annotators make?', 'Do I need experience?', 'What is RLHF?', 'How does Claru pay annotators?', 'Can I work remotely?'
Use existing FAQItem component (collapsible accordion)
Each answer starts with a direct statement before elaboration (answer-first AEO pattern)
Inject FAQPage JSON-LD schema in page head via script tag
Schema includes @context, @type FAQPage, mainEntity array with Question/Answer pairs
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. 8 FAQ items with answer-first format, FAQPage JSON-LD schema in layout.tsx.

userStories.title: Internal linking between supply and demand pages
userStories.description: As an SEO system, I need hub-and-spoke internal linking so that authority flows between supply pages and demand pages.
acceptanceCriteria: /work-with-us links to /jobs listing page
/work-with-us links to /pillars/enrich/expert-annotation with contextual anchor text
/work-with-us links to /data and /labeling pages (e.g., 'See how our data is used')
Add breadcrumb-style navigation component for supply-side pages (Home > Work With Us)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. 3 internal link cards (Training Data, Expert Labeling, All Positions) with hover effects. Breadcrumbs already present.

userStories.title: /jobs listing page with search and category filters
userStories.description: As a potential annotator, I want to browse and search available positions so that I can find roles matching my skills.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/jobs/page.tsx
Page metadata: title 'AI Annotation Jobs | Remote Data Labeling Opportunities at Claru'
Search input filters jobs by keyword across title, skills, and description
Category filter pills: All, Data Labeling, Quality Review, Video Capture, Gaming, RLHF, Red Teaming, Coding Review, Vision Annotation
Job count display: 'Showing X of Y positions'
Job cards in grid layout: title, category tag, up to 3 skills, compensation range ($X-Y/hr), Remote badge, date posted, Apply button
Apply button on each card links to https://app.claru.ai/signup
Each card also links to /jobs/[slug] for full details
Pagination or Load more when >18 jobs
Uses shared Header/Footer, matches ASCII aesthetic with TextScramble on heading

userStories.title: Individual job detail page with JobPosting schema
userStories.description: As a potential annotator, I want to view full job details on a dedicated page. As a search engine, I need JobPosting structured data to surface this in Google Jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/jobs/[slug]/page.tsx with generateStaticParams for static generation
Page displays: full title, category badge, description (rendered markdown), skills list, compensation range, location type, date posted
Prominent Apply Now CTA button linking to https://app.claru.ai/signup
Answer block near top: 2-3 sentence summary of the role (AEO-ready)
FAQ section specific to job category (reusable across same-category jobs)
Related positions section showing 3-4 jobs from same category
Internal link to relevant demand-side pillar page (e.g., RLHF job links to /pillars/enrich/rlhf)

JobPosting JSON-LD schema in head: title, description, datePosted, validThrough, employmentType, hiringOrganization (name: Claru, sameAs: https://claru.ai), jobLocationType: TELECOMMUTE, baseSalary with min/max hourly USD
Open Graph and Twitter meta tags for social sharing
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. 44 static job pages with JobPosting JSON-LD, answer block, FAQ accordion, related jobs, OG/Twitter meta. Build passes.

userStories.title: About Claru sidebar and Share button on job detail pages
userStories.description: As a potential annotator, I want to see company context and share job listings so that I can learn about Claru and spread the word.
acceptanceCriteria: Add AboutClaruSidebar component: Claru logo (small), 2-3 sentence company description, key stats, links (Learn more -> /, All positions -> /jobs, Our services -> /data)
Sidebar renders on right side on desktop, collapses below content on mobile

Add ShareButton component with 4 options: Copy link (copies URL to clipboard with toast 'Link copied'), LinkedIn (opens share dialog), Twitter/X (opens tweet composer with title + URL), Email (mailto with subject + body)
Both components render on /jobs/[slug] pages
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — test share copy, LinkedIn link, Twitter link, email link
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. AboutClaruSidebar + ShareButton components with clipboard, LinkedIn, Twitter, Email, accessibility, animations.

userStories.title: RSS feed for job listings
userStories.description: As a job board aggregator or RSS reader, I need an RSS feed of Claru's job listings for automatic syndication.
acceptanceCriteria: Create RSS 2.0 route handler at src/app/jobs/feed.xml/route.ts
Feed channel: title 'Claru AI Jobs', link to /jobs, description, language en-us
Each active (non-archived) job is an <item> with: title, link (/jobs/[slug]), description (first 200 chars), pubDate (datePosted), category, guid (slug)
Feed sorted by datePosted descending (newest first)
Add <link rel='alternate' type='application/rss+xml' href='/jobs/feed.xml'> to /jobs page head
Route returns proper Content-Type: application/xml header
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. RSS 2.0 feed at /jobs/feed.xml with XML escaping, CDATA, atom:link, 1hr cache. Excludes archived.

userStories.title: Admin authentication with login page and middleware
userStories.description: As a Claru team member, I need a login page at /admin so that only authorized users can access the job management portal.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/page.tsx with login form (email + password fields)
Login form styled in ASCII/terminal aesthetic: monospace, dark theme, green accent input borders
Create /api/admin/login API route that validates credentials against ADMIN_EMAIL and ADMIN_PASSWORD env vars
On valid login, set httpOnly JWT cookie with 24hr expiry (use JWT_SECRET env var for signing)
On invalid login, return 401 and show inline error: 'Invalid credentials'
Create middleware.ts (or update existing) to protect all /admin/* routes — redirect to /admin if no valid session
Add logout functionality: clear cookie and redirect to /admin
Add noindex meta tag and exclude /admin/* from sitemap
Create .env.local.example with ADMIN_EMAIL, ADMIN_PASSWORD, JWT_SECRET placeholders
NEVER hardcode credentials — only read from env vars
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — test login, redirect, logout
Run code-reviewer agent — verify no hardcoded credentials
userStories.notes: Completed. Login page, API routes, middleware, JWT auth with jose, admin layout all created. Build passes.

userStories.title: Admin job listing dashboard
userStories.description: As a Claru team member, I want to see all job listings in a sortable table so that I can manage the job board content.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/jobs/page.tsx (protected route)
Table with columns: Title, Category, Compensation, Date Posted, Status (Active/Archived), Actions
Table sortable by clicking column headers
Search input filters jobs by title or category
Status badge: green 'Active' or gray 'Archived'
Job count summary at top: 'X active, Y archived, Z total'
Actions column has Edit and Archive/Unarchive buttons (wire up in later stories)
Terminal aesthetic: monospace table text, dark theme, subtle borders
Responsive: table scrolls horizontally on mobile
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Run code-reviewer agent on all changed files
userStories.notes: Completed. Server component loads jobs, client AdminJobsTable with sort/search/filter/status badges. Terminal aesthetic.

userStories.title: Archive and unarchive jobs from admin
userStories.description: As a Claru team member, I want to archive jobs so they stop appearing on the public job board, and unarchive them to bring them back.
acceptanceCriteria: Create /api/admin/jobs/[slug]/archive API route (POST) that toggles archived field in the job's JSON file
API route reads current JSON, flips archived boolean, writes back
Archive button shows confirmation prompt: 'Archive [job title]? It will be hidden from the public job board.'
On success show toast: 'Job archived' or 'Job restored'
Table row updates status badge immediately after action
Public /jobs page and /jobs/[slug] pages filter out archived jobs (already handled by getAllJobs default)
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — archive a job, check it disappears from public /jobs, unarchive it, check it reappears
Run code-reviewer agent — verify file write operations are safe
userStories.notes: Completed. Archive API route + UI wiring with confirmation and toast. Build passes.

userStories.title: Edit job details from admin
userStories.description: As a Claru team member, I want to edit a job's details so that I can correct or improve listings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/admin/jobs/[slug]/edit/page.tsx
Edit form pre-populated with current job data: title, category (dropdown), description (textarea), skills (tag input), compensationMin, compensationMax, locationRequirements, validThrough
Form validation: title required, compensationMin < compensationMax, at least 1 skill, max 5 skills
Description textarea has markdown live preview (side-by-side on desktop, tabbed on mobile)
Skills input: type to add, click X to remove
Slug and datePosted are NOT editable
Create /api/admin/jobs/[slug] API route (PUT) that writes updated data to JSON file
After save, redirect to /admin/jobs with success toast: 'Job updated'
Cancel button returns to /admin/jobs without saving
Terminal aesthetic: monospace inputs, green focus borders, dark background
npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill — edit a job title, save, verify it persists
Run code-reviewer agent — verify input sanitization, no XSS in markdown rendering
userStories.notes: Completed. Edit form with tag input, validation, PUT API route, AdminJobForm component. Build passes.

userStories.title: Claude Agent SDK job scraping agent - tool definitions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the tool definitions and agent scaffold for the job scraping agent so that it can be wired up to scrape and save jobs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create scripts/job-agent.ts entry point using @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk
Add @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk and playwright to package.json devDependencies

Define 5 tools: scrape_job_board (Playwright scrapes micro1.ai/jobs, returns raw job array), check_duplicate (fuzzy title match + category against existing jobs), save_job (validates Job schema, writes JSON to src/data/jobs/), list_existing_jobs (reads all slugs/titles/categories), get_claru_categories (returns valid categories + compensation tiers)
Each tool has proper input/output JSON schema definitions
Agent system prompt defines role: 'You are Claru's job board curator. Find AI annotation job postings, adapt them into Claru positions, and save them.'
System prompt includes constraints: answer-first descriptions, anti-AI-ness rules, compensation normalization tiers, category mapping rules, FAQ generation per job
Configurable max token budget (default 100k) to prevent runaway costs
Agent gracefully handles errors without crashing
Runnable locally: npx tsx scripts/job-agent.ts
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent — verify tool definitions, error handling, token budget enforcement
userStories.notes: Completed. 5 tools defined (scrape, list, check_duplicate, save, get_categories), 100k token budget, Playwright scraping, fuzzy dedup. Build passes.

userStories.title: GitHub Action for weekly job scraping
userStories.description: As a system operator, I need the scraping agent to run automatically on a weekly schedule.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .github/workflows/scrape-jobs.yml
Runs on weekly cron: '0 6 * * 1' (Monday 6am UTC)
Also triggerable via workflow_dispatch

Steps: checkout, install deps (npm ci), install Playwright browsers (npx playwright install chromium), run agent (npx tsx scripts/job-agent.ts), commit new job files, push to branch, create PR via gh cli
Requires secrets: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
PR title: 'chore: add new job listings [automated]'
PR body includes summary of new jobs added
Does NOT auto-merge — requires human review
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent — verify workflow security, secrets handling
userStories.notes: Completed. Weekly cron + manual dispatch, Playwright install, PR creation with review checklist. YAML validated.

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test - Job Board Browse and Apply Flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need an end-to-end test verifying job board browsing, searching, filtering, detail view, and apply flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/job-board.spec.ts
Test: navigate to /jobs, verify job cards render with title, category, skills, compensation
Test: type search term, verify results filter correctly
Test: click category filter, verify jobs filtered by category
Test: click job card, verify navigation to /jobs/[slug]
Test: on detail page verify title, description, skills, compensation, About Claru sidebar, Share button
Test: verify JobPosting JSON-LD exists in page source
Test: verify Apply Now links to app.claru.ai/signup
Test: navigate to /work-with-us, verify hub page renders with all sections
Test uses Page Object Model pattern

Run code-reviewer agent on test file
userStories.notes: Completed. 12 tests: listing, search, filters, detail page, schema, breadcrumbs, skills, apply link, sidebar, hub page, FAQ. All 12 passed (5.1s).

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test - SEO Schema and RSS Validation
userStories.description: As QA, I need tests verifying JobPosting structured data and RSS feed compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/job-seo.spec.ts
Test: for 5 sample job pages, extract and parse JSON-LD JobPosting schema
Test: assert required fields: title, description, datePosted, validThrough, hiringOrganization, jobLocationType, baseSalary
Test: assert hiringOrganization.name === 'Claru'
Test: assert jobLocationType === 'TELECOMMUTE'
Test: assert baseSalary values within expected ranges ($20-100/hr)
Test: verify FAQPage schema exists on /work-with-us
Test: fetch /jobs/feed.xml, verify valid XML with RSS 2.0 structure
Test: verify RSS item count matches active job count
Typecheck passes
Run code-reviewer agent on test file
userStories.notes: Completed. 3 tests: JobPosting schema validation, FAQPage schema, RSS feed XML. All 3 passed (7.4s).

userStories.title: Playwright E2E Test - Admin Portal Flow
userStories.description: As QA, I need tests verifying admin login, dashboard, archive, and edit workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Create tests/e2e/admin-portal.spec.ts
Test: navigate to /admin, verify login form renders
Test: submit invalid credentials, verify error message
Test: submit valid credentials (from env), verify redirect to /admin/jobs
Test: verify job table renders with correct columns and data
Test: search for job by title, verify filter works
Test: archive a job, confirm dialog, verify status changes to Archived
Test: unarchive same job, verify status returns to Active
Test: click Edit, verify form pre-populated, change title, save, verify updated on dashboard
Test: logout, verify redirect to /admin login, verify /admin/jobs redirects to login
Test uses Page Object Model pattern
